_comment: Reference knowledge-base.article.create body for tawk.to. Exercises every verified block type (header, paragraph with inline HTML, unordered + ordered lists, blockquote, image, code, divider, table, video). VERIFIED by a live create+update on 2026-06-05. Replace PROPERTY_ID, AUTHOR_AGENT_ID, CATEGORY_ID, RELATED_ARTICLE_ID and the hosted image URLs. POST to https://api.tawk.to/v1/knowledge-base.article.create with Authorization: Basic base64(apiKey + ':').
_gotchas: 1) Do NOT send article.content.metadata on CREATE - both {banner,description} and {description} returned validation_error 'article.content.metadata'. Omit it (set description/banner via a later update once the accepted shape is known). 2) video.content.options is REQUIRED, and startTime/endTime are NUMBERS (seconds), not strings as the public docs claim. For source 'youtube' the full flag set is required: startTime, endTime, autoplay, mobile, mute, controls, loop, branding, privacy. 3) categories/relatedArticles must reference real ids or be []; placeholder ids fail. 4) meta.categories[] entries must be {id, primary} ONLY - including a 'name' field returns validation_error 'article.meta.categories[].name' on create AND update.

content.title: Install the Sight pixel
content.subtitle: Add the tracking snippet to your site in under five minutes
content.author: AUTHOR_AGENT_ID

content.text: Who this is for: Owners and admins setting up a new site. Takes about five minutes.

The Sight pixel is a small script that identifies the companies and people visiting your site. Once it is firing, your visitor feeds start to populate. You can read the full reference in the pixel docs.

• You are an Owner or Admin on the account.
  • You can edit your site's HTML, or you use a tag manager.
  • Your consent banner is configured (or you are exempt).

1. Open Settings > Install the script in the portal.
  2. Copy your unique snippet (it includes your site key).
  3. Paste it into the <head> of every page, just before the closing </head> tag.
  4. Publish your site and reload it once.
Your snippet looks like this (replace YOUR_SITE_KEY with the value from the portal):

<!-- Sight by Legion -->
<script>
  (function (s, i, g, h, t) {
    s[t] = s[t] || function () { (s[t].q = s[t].q || []).push(arguments); };
    var a = i.createElement(g), m = i.getElementsByTagName(g)[0];
    a.async = 1; a.src = h; m.parentNode.insertBefore(a, m);
  })(window, document, 'script', 'https://pixel.legionsight.ai/s.js', 'sight');
  sight('init', 'YOUR_SITE_KEY');
</script>
If you use Google Tag Manager instead, add it as a Custom HTML tag firing on All Pages.
Use the table below to read the install status shown on the Install the script page.

cells.value: The pixel fired on your domain. You are done.
No event received yet. Reload your site, then re-check.
A consent or ad blocker stopped the script. Check your consent banner.

content.text: Tip: it can take a minute for the first event to land. If status stays Not detected after a reload, confirm the snippet is in the <head> and that your site published.
